_meta.purpose: Known issues, bugs, and their current status — updated after each audit/fix cycle

bugs.title: In-memory rate limiting resets on server restart
bugs.description: The failed-scan guard uses a plain JavaScript Map in backend/index.js. This state is lost on every server restart or crash. An attacker can abuse scan endpoints by triggering a server restart to reset the block.
bugs.fix_approach: Replace with Redis-backed store using express-rate-limit ioredis store or a persistent DB flag

bugs.title: No CSRF protection on state-mutating endpoints
bugs.description: All POST/PATCH/DELETE endpoints lack CSRF token validation. Express JSON body parsing does not constitute CSRF protection. Organizer session (accessCode held in localStorage/URL) is stateless but cross-site form submissions can still trigger unintended mutations.
bugs.fix_approach: Add csurf middleware or implement SameSite=Strict cookies with double-submit cookie pattern

bugs.title: No schema validation on public endpoints — ticketSelections unvalidated
bugs.description: POST /api/public/ticket-request accepts ticketSelections as an array with no Zod/Joi validation. Malformed input (wrong types, negative quantities, missing fields) reaches the controller and can cause unhandled errors or corrupt data.
bugs.fix_approach: Add Zod schemas for all public endpoint bodies; validate before controller logic runs

bugs.title: Zero test coverage — regressions undetectable
bugs.description: The entire backend and frontend have 0% test coverage. No unit tests, no integration tests, no E2E tests. Every commit has an unknown regression risk. Critical paths (scan, approval, OTP) are entirely untested.
bugs.fix_approach: Start with integration tests for 3 critical paths: scan flow, ticket request + approval, OTP verification

bugs.title: No structured logging — production errors are invisible
bugs.description: Backend uses console.error() for all error logging. Morgan logs HTTP requests but not application errors, slow queries, or business logic failures. When something breaks in production, there is no queryable, alertable record.
bugs.fix_approach: Add Winston logger with JSON transport; replace all console.error/console.log with logger.error/logger.info

bugs.title: No CI/CD pipeline — deploys are fully manual
bugs.description: .github/ folder contains only a PR template. Zero GitHub Actions workflows. Every deploy requires manual SSH + docker compose commands. No automated tests run before deploy. No rollback automation.
bugs.fix_approach: Add .github/workflows/ci.yml (lint + test + audit) and .github/workflows/deploy.yml (deploy on main push)

bugs.title: organizerAccessCode case-sensitivity was a latent bug
bugs.description: chatService.js normalizeAccessCode() was calling .toUpperCase() before DB lookup. organizerAccessCode is stored mixed-case in Postgres (case-sensitive), causing lookup failures. Fixed by removing .toUpperCase() (commit in 2026-03-18 session).

bugs.title: ticketPrice null recovery was fragile
bugs.description: When ticketPrice was null on a ticket, the system failed to display price in client dashboard. Fixed to fall back to event config ticketPrice (commit 3c9e6b8).
bugs.fix_approach: Already fixed — recover from event config on null

bugs.title: No error tracking — errors go unreported unless manually discovered
bugs.description: No Sentry, Rollbar, or equivalent error tracking. Runtime exceptions in production are invisible unless an organizer or buyer reports them. No alerting on 5xx error rates.
bugs.fix_approach: Add @sentry/node with Express error handler; configure DSN via environment variable

bugs.title: Share Event button state not persisted across refresh
bugs.description: The draggable Share Event button resets its position on refresh (by design per commit 54927fc). This is intentional but should be documented as a UX decision if persistence is ever requested.
bugs.fix_approach: If persistence is desired: save position to localStorage keyed by accessCode

bugs.title: Chat attachments stored outside Docker volume — data loss on container rebuild
bugs.description: backend/services/chatAttachmentService.js stores files at path.resolve(__dirname, '..', 'storage', 'private', 'chat') which resolves to /app/storage/private/chat in the container. The production docker-compose.yml maps /opt/qr-tickets/uploads -> /app/uploads. These are different paths. Chat attachments (images, PDFs) are NOT persisted — they are lost on every container rebuild or restart.
bugs.fix_approach: Either: (a) change CHAT_STORAGE_ROOT to /app/uploads/private/chat and create that path, or (b) add a second volume mount for /app/storage in docker-compose.yml

bugs.title: Recovery OTP and contact form endpoints have no rate limiting
bugs.description: backend/index.js applies rate limiting to /api/public/send-otp, /api/public/verify-otp, and /api/public/recover-client-token — but NOT to: /api/public/recover-organizer-code/send-otp, /api/public/recover-organizer-code/confirm, /api/public/contact/send-otp, /api/public/contact. These endpoints send emails and can be abused for email bombing or brute-force OTP attacks.
bugs.fix_approach: Add otpLimiter to /api/public/recover-organizer-code/* and a contactLimiter to /api/public/contact/*

bugs.title: Scan outcome values in docs/memory don't match actual API responses
bugs.description: API contract and memory_product.json document scan outcomes as: VALID, USED, INVALID, EXPIRED. Actual API returns: VALID, ALREADY_USED, INVALID_TICKET, EXPIRED, BLOCKED. DB stores: VALID, USED, INVALID (mapped from internal outcomes). The BLOCKED outcome (scanner locked by admin) is undocumented everywhere. Frontend may handle these correctly but docs are misleading for future development.
bugs.fix_approach: Update docs/API_CONTRACT.md scan outcomes section to reflect actual returned values. Update memory_product.json scan_flow.outcomes.

bugs.title: npm dependency CVEs — 6 HIGH in backend, 2 in frontend
bugs.description: npm audit results: Backend has 8 vulnerabilities (1 low, 1 moderate, 6 high). Key issues: path-to-regexp ReDoS vulnerability (GHSA-37ch-88jc-xwx2) in Express routing, picomatch ReDoS. Frontend has 2 vulnerabilities (1 moderate, 1 high): picomatch ReDoS, yaml stack overflow. All fixable via npm audit fix.
bugs.fix_approach: Run npm audit fix in both backend/ and frontend/. Verify no breaking changes after upgrade.
